checks.name: Invalid port -> exit 2 with stderr mentioning env var (R0 + R4 hardening)
checks.command: HOMELAB_MCP_HTTP_PORT=garbage uv run homelab-mcp-http  ;  HOMELAB_MCP_HTTP_PORT=70000 uv run homelab-mcp-http
checks.evidence: Both non-integer and out-of-range ports exit 2 with diagnostic stderr line citing HOMELAB_MCP_HTTP_PORT

checks.name: FastAPI tests (healthz/metrics/auth/no-auth/zero-tools/manager-error/trailing-slash)
categories: healthz happy + degraded (zero-tools 503, manager-error 503 with reason)
metrics prom format on canonical and trailing-slash paths
auth: 401 + WWW-Authenticate, RFC bearer scheme case-insensitivity, valid-token pass-through, no-auth path, healthz always open
trailing-slash variants /healthz/, /metrics/ open with token set
run_uvicorn: token strip, whitespace-only fail-closed, port range parametrised

deferred.name: Image build + dual smoke (F-009, F-012)
deferred.reason: Local docker not installed on this dev box. CI build-monolith.yml on push will produce the image. Dual smoke (default ENTRYPOINT serves /healthz; --entrypoint homelab-mcp serves stdio) MUST be verified manually after the GHCR build, before bumping the cluster chart.
manual_steps: 1. After CI tags the image (sha-XXXXXXX), pull it on home.hont.ro: docker pull ghcr.io/dragoshont/homelab-mcp:sha-XXXXXXX

2. (HTTP) docker run --rm -d -p 8080:8080 ghcr.io/dragoshont/homelab-mcp:sha-XXXXXXX ; curl http://localhost:8080/healthz -> expect 200, body has status='ok' and tools > 0 (do NOT pin a specific count; tool registry can grow)
3. (stdio) echo '{init-payload}' | docker run --rm -i --entrypoint homelab-mcp ghcr.io/dragoshont/homelab-mcp:sha-XXXXXXX -> expect serverInfo.name='homelab'
4. ONLY THEN bump the chart tag in homelab repo.

notes: Updated post-shipment to reflect 6 verify rounds + R5 hardening + PR review fixes (WWW-Authenticate header, doc/artifact reconciliation).
tools=N expectation in T-A9 step 2 is intentionally NOT pinned to 133 - registry can grow over time.
